page: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. This x86_64 architecture server provides 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation and a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and SHA_NI. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scoring in the top tier (top 10%). Passmark memory performance also ranks in the top tier. Large-block uncached memory bandwidth (both read and write) and single-threaded gzip compression perform in the strong top 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core metrics, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and Passmark CPU scores, fall into the average middle 50% range. This performance profile suggests a potential tradeoff where single-threaded execution and memory access efficiency outpace parallelized multi-core scaling under shared CPU allocation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a balanced resource density, pairing 64.0 GB of RAM with 320 GB of NVMe storage on a shared-core model. This configuration reduces the premium typically associated with dedicated-core instances while maintaining high memory bandwidth. The instance is suitable for workloads that benefit from strong single-core execution and high memory throughput, such as database operations, static web serving, and single-threaded data processing tasks, whereas highly parallelized, sustained multi-core compute tasks may experience resource contention due to the shared CPU allocation.
description: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 16 vCPUs with shared CPU allocation, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in single-core stress-ng tests, single-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Passmark memory scores. Large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded gzip compression rank in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU benchmarks and database operations fall into the average tier, indicating a tradeoff typical of shared CPU allocations. This server provides a cost-effective option for workloads requiring high memory bandwidth and strong single-threaded performance, such as web serving, database operations, and moderate data processing, without the expense of dedicated CPU cores.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server with 16 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 320 GB NVMe storage, delivering top-tier single-core and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd features 16 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 320 GB NVMe SSD storage with shared CPU allocation.
tagline: A shared-core AMD EPYC server balancing top-tier single-threaded performance and high memory bandwidth with local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 16 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs with shared CPU allocation
64.0 GB system memory and 320 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth
Suited for web serving, database operations, and single-threaded processing
